expectations.description: DashboardLayout이 folder 기반 index.tsx 패턴으로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: components/DashboardLayout/index.tsx exists as the main entry point, with sub-components Header.tsx, Sidebar.tsx, MobileNav.tsx, and navLinks.ts organized inside the DashboardLayout folder.
No flat components/DashboardLayout.tsx file exists in the outputs. Only the folder-based components/DashboardLayout/index.tsx pattern is present.
components/DashboardLayout/Sidebar.tsx contains a dedicated <aside> element with data-testid='dashboard-sidebar', featuring navigation links, logo, and footer section.
components/DashboardLayout/Header.tsx contains a dedicated <header> element with data-testid='dashboard-header', sticky positioning, title, and right slot for actions.
index.tsx renders a <main> element with data-testid='dashboard-main' and className='flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-4 py-6 pb-20 md:px-6 md:pb-6 lg:px-8' that wraps {children}.

DashboardLayout accepts children: ReactNode and renders {children} inside the main content area. The page.tsx consumer passes content as children: <DashboardLayout headerTitle='대시보드' currentPath='/dashboard'>...content...</DashboardLayout>.

expectations.description: responsive breakpoint class가 유지되거나 개선된다 (md:, lg: 등)
expectations.evidence: Multiple responsive breakpoints used: Sidebar has 'hidden md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64', Header uses 'md:px-6' and 'md:hidden'/'md:block' for mobile/desktop switching, MobileNav uses 'md:hidden', main content uses 'md:px-6 md:pb-6 lg:px-8'.

expectations.description: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: app/dashboard/page.tsx imports from '@/components/DashboardLayout' which resolves to the folder's index.tsx. This is the correct import path for the folder-based pattern.

expectations.description: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Grep search across all files in components/DashboardLayout/ found zero matches for useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation. The layout components are pure presentational shells with no data-fetching logic.

summary: All 9 expectations PASS. The DashboardLayout was properly refactored into a folder-based pattern (components/DashboardLayout/index.tsx) with clearly separated Sidebar, Header, MobileNav, and navLinks sub-components. No flat DashboardLayout.tsx remains. The layout serves as a pure children-based shell with responsive Tailwind breakpoints (md:, lg:) and no data-fetching logic. The consumer import path is correctly updated.
claims: DashboardLayout uses folder-based index.tsx pattern with 4 sub-modules
Sidebar is a dedicated aside element with nav links
Header is a dedicated sticky header element with title and action slots
Main content area renders children in a flex-1 main element
Layout is a pure shell accepting children ReactNode
Responsive breakpoints md: and lg: are used throughout
Import path @/components/DashboardLayout correctly resolves to folder index
No data-fetching hooks or calls in any layout component
eval_feedback: The without_skill output produces a well-structured folder-based DashboardLayout. All expectations are met. The decomposition into Sidebar, Header, MobileNav, and navLinks is clean. One minor note: the page.tsx (not a layout component) does use useEffect for auth redirect, but expectation 9 applies to the layout components themselves, which are free of data-fetching calls.
